subtitle: Hip or glute pain — location not pinpointed

strava_risk_triggers.risk_note: General hip pain often stems from glute weakness and mileage spikes

mild.description: Vague aching in the hip or glute region after running
mild.can_run_note: Yes — reduce intensity and add hip strengthening
why: General hip muscle fatigue from mileage increase
Weak glute complex failing to stabilise the pelvis under load
Tight hip capsule and external rotators from sustained running
relief: Ice or heat depending on whether the pain feels sharp or achy
prevention: Full hip strengthening programme: glute bridge, clamshell, lateral walk
Do not increase mileage more than 10% per week

moderate.description: Consistent hip pain affecting running form
moderate.can_run_note: Short easy runs only — identify the specific structure before progressing
why: Multiple possible structures involved — needs specific diagnosis
relief: Reduce mileage by 50% and eliminate speed and hill work
Try to identify whether pain is anterior, lateral, or posterior
Rest and assess — use this app to try to narrow down the location
prevention: Targeted hip strengthening based on specific structure identified
Gait analysis to identify compensatory patterns

severe.description: Significant hip pain limiting walking or daily activities
severe.can_run_note: Do not run — seek diagnosis first
why: Undiagnosed hip pathology requiring proper assessment
Possible hip labral tear, stress fracture, or joint pathology
Pain at this level needs imaging to guide treatment
relief: Rest and see a physiotherapist or sports medicine doctor
Do not self-treat significant hip pain without diagnosis
Imaging including X-ray or MRI may be needed
prevention: Return to running only with a clear diagnosis and structured plan

see_physio_if: Pain in the groin suggesting hip joint involvement
Pain at night or at rest
Clicking or locking in the hip joint
No improvement after 1 week of reduced load
